label: What kind of home atmosphere do you prefer?
I prefer a quiet home (rare group hangs).
How many guests do you expect to have per week?
Excluding rare events like birthdays, what is the maximum number of people that should be in the apartment at any given time before it feels overwhelming?

options.label: Just us and maybe 1 - 2 partners (Max 4 people)
A small dinner party size (Max 6 - 8 people)
The more the merrier (10+ people is fine)

label: What is your maximum acceptable limit for a housemate's romantic partner sleeping over during the workweek?

options.label: 0 - 1 night per week
2 - 3 nights per week
4+ nights per week

label: When your housemate has friends over in the living room, how do you prefer to handle the situation?

options.label: I briefly say hello and then retreat to my room to give them space
I hang out for a drink or chat, then do my own thing
I actively want to be included and hang out with them the whole time

label: Do you prefer to keep your friend group strictly separate from your housemate's friend group, or do you actively want to merge social lives?

options.label: Keep strictly separate
Actively merge social lives

label: Latest gathering end (weeknights)
How do you feel about hosting daytime events (e.g., Sunday brunches, watching sports) on the weekends?

options.label: Not a fan; weekends are my quiet recovery time
Once in a while is great, with advance notice
I love it; weekends should be highly social and active

label: When hosting events at the apartment, what is your preferred scale?
I require advance notice (e.g., a text in the group chat) if guests will be in the common areas during weekday evenings.
I'm willing to switch to the common language when someone joins.
I believe there should be a strict limit on how many nights per week a romantic partner or guest can stay over before they must contribute to household expenses/utilities.
I am comfortable with my housemate having out-of-town friends or family sleep on the living room couch for a weekend.
If you want to invite 2 - 3 friends over for dinner on a Tuesday night, what is the protocol?

options.label: Ask for explicit permission at least 24 hours in advance
Give a heads-up via text earlier that day
Just bring them over; no notice required

label: What is the maximum duration an out-of-town guest should be allowed to stay continuously in the apartment?

options.label: 1 - 2 nights max.
3 - 5 nights (long weekend)
1 - 2 weeks (as long as they are respectful)

label: I prefer a no smoking or vaping household. Smoking and vaping should only happen outside and away from windows.
What role does alcohol/drinking play in your ideal home environment?
I plan to frequently host colleagues, clients, or professional contacts at the apartment for dinners or meetings.
It bothers me if a housemate's partner is frequently hanging out in the shared living room while my housemate is not home (e.g., they are at work or at the gym)
If a housemate's partner stays over regularly, do you expect them to start contributing to household utilities or rent?

options.label: Yes, financial boundaries must be set
No, they are a guest

label: If my housemate has guests who speak a language I do not understand, I expect them to switch to a shared language (e.g., English) when I enter the room.
After gatherings, quiet hours apply immediately.
When my bedroom door is closed, it means I am completely off-limits for social interaction, regardless of the time of day.
I believe it is perfectly acceptable to give a spare key to my romantic partner or best friend so they can let themselves in when I am not home.
